opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My body feels like lead, my brain has stopped working entirely, and I'm in constant, deep muscle pain.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: I've crashed badly after overdoing it two days ago — I can't stand or think and my spouse is helping me.

symptom_narrative.onset: a crash over the last two days after over-exertion
symptom_narrative.character: leaden paralytic fatigue, deep muscle pain, and thick brain fog/slow speech
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: whole-body heaviness and deep muscle pain
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: post-exertional crash, not relieved by sleep
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: deep myalgia and cognitive failure; no fever
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I have post-Q-fever fatigue and crash, but this is severe

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: Q Fever Fatigue Syndrome after documented Q fever 2 years ago

family_history_as_reported.narrative: nothing relevant that I know of

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: rural agricultural worker; spouse is caring for me

objective_data_offered.value: spouse reports his face is even, both arms lift equally (weakly), speech slow but not slurred/one-sided
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: informal spouse-performed FAST, not a neuro exam

objective_data_offered.value: he wakes to voice and can whisper answers
